summary: All 5 codex-review-1.json blocking findings verified resolved by direct text comparison plus ground-truth spot-checks against the clone at /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-anicca-anicca-project/ec3606df-8de7-491a-8a92-7ee667020d6a/scratchpad/blockrun-mcp. (1) REQ-017 BLOCKRUN_HOME: grepped both spec files, BLOCKRUN_HOME appears exactly once, in REQ-017 itself, stating a single non-contradictory rule (no BLOCKRUN_HOME support; hermetic isolation via spawning with HOME set) -- no residual contradictory reference anywhere else. (2) Schema fidelity / agent_id: grepped all 18 src/tools/*.ts -- 16 files reference agent_id (all except models.ts and dex.ts), spot-checked chat/defi/exa/image/markets/modal/music/phone/price/realface/rpc/search/speech/surf/video.ts -- every one declares `agent_id: z.string().optional()` as a top-level canonical inputSchema field; wallet.ts scopes agent_id/agent_limit to delegate/revoke/report only. This matches REQ-022's claim exactly ('all 18 tools except wallet, models, dex' = 15 have it as a plain top-level field, wallet has it scoped). search.ts's actual inputSchema is path/body/agent_id (REQ-152 canonical) with query/sources/max_results/from_date/to_date correctly demoted to REQ-152a aliases matching the tool description's 'Common shape' block verbatim; same check on exa.ts confirms REQ-154/154a's canonical path/body/agent_id + per-path alias tables match the tool description exactly. video.ts and phone.ts full inputSchema field lists match REQ-130 and REQ-175 1:1. (3) Budget controls: REQ-018 now defines --budget-limit as a GLOBAL flag with a single stated precedence (flag > BLOCKRUN_BUDGET_LIMIT env > unlimited), explicitly EPHEMERAL/never touching the persisted ledger; REQ-020/REQ-220 both reference REQ-018 and REQ-019c consistently, no orphaned --budget-limit mention found (grepped). REQ-019/019a/019b/019c define a persisted ~/.blockrun/cli-budget.json ledger with schema, atomic tmp-then-rename write, and cross-invocation enforcement -- resolves the prior 'admits state does not survive the one-shot process model' problem. (4) Tier-3 wallet delegate+report roundtrip: PROP-103 in verification-architecture.md now explicitly requires 'real delegate in invocation A, THEN real report in a SEPARATE, subsequent CLI invocation B -- asserting B's output reflects the allocation A wrote to ~/.blockrun/cli-budget.json' -- executable now that state.json is disk-persisted rather than in-memory. (5) Video quote gate: REQ-135a, PROP-114(b), PROP-115, and verification-architecture.md section 4.3's video row all state an IDENTICAL rule using matching numbers -- non-interactive --max-quote-usd flag, abort BEFORE createPaymentPayload() with no signature ever produced over the cap, nonzero exit, exact quote surfaced in both human and --json forms; example invocation and $0.10 threshold match verbatim across all four locations. New requirements (REQ-004a, 019a-c, 022, 023, 107a, 135a, 152a, 154a; PROP-011a, 205, 206) are each EARS-well-formed (WHEN/SHALL or ubiquitous SHALL form), testable, and tier-assigned in verification-architecture.md's traceability tables; none contradict an existing REQ. Additional ground-truth spot-checks not in the codex list, all confirmed byte-accurate: realface.ts name z.string().min(1).max(64) and image_url generic z.string().url() (REQ-137); video.ts azure/sora-2 duration_seconds-in-{4,8,12} is prose-only, not a zod enum (REQ-132); music.ts's txHash-gated recordActualSpend-before-throw at lines 190-196 (REQ-146); surf.ts SURF_T2_PATHS hand-counted at exactly 19 entries (REQ-179). One MINOR (non-blocking) finding: REQ-019a states BLOCKRUN_BUDGET_LIMIT seeds the persisted global.limit only 'WHEN the file does not yet exist' -- the spec never states what happens to the persisted global.limit if a caller changes BLOCKRUN_BUDGET_LIMIT in a later invocation after the ledger file already exists (implicitly: no effect, since REQ-019c's re-read/re-write logic only touches the persisted value via wallet budget set/clear or spend accounting, never re-seeds from env). Recommend adding one clause to REQ-019a stating this explicitly so an implementer/verifier doesn't have to infer it. Holistic pass: a competent implementer/verifier could execute this contract end-to-end without needing to guess about money-spending behavior -- the three independent budget gates (ephemeral --budget-limit, persisted global/per-agent ledger, video's --max-quote-usd) are each defined once and referenced consistently everywhere they interact, and the video quote-gate (the highest-risk real-money path) is now a fully deterministic, non-interactive, product-code-enforced rule with no blind-signing step. Phase 1c gate: PASS -- clear to proceed to vcsdd-tdd.
